title: Isaiah, Chapter 25
description: Isaiah chapter 25. Both the manuscript page scan AND the Greek text are taken directly from Codex Sinaiticus (4th century AD) via the Codex Sinaiticus Project (codexsinaiticus.org). The Greek transcription preserves the actual uncial forms, lunate sigma, nomina sacra abbreviations, and lectional marks of the manuscript. Library folio: 51. English translation verbatim from the King James Version (1611, public domain) via bible-api.com.
introduction:
This entry meets the strict three-criteria standard of the Theosis Library, with the manuscript text drawn directly from the same source as the scan:

  1. Manuscript scan: The actual folio of Codex Sinaiticus containing this passage, from the Codex Sinaiticus Project (codexsinaiticus.org). Library folio reference: 51.
  2. Original text: The verbatim transcription of the Sinaiticus manuscript from the Codex Sinaiticus Project, preserving uncial forms, lunate sigma, nomina sacra (e.g. ⟨θϲ⟩ = θεός), and scribal marks. Nomina sacra are shown in angle brackets to indicate the manuscript's sacred-name abbreviations.
  3. English translation: The King James Version (1611), retrieved verbatim from the public-domain bible-api.com endpoint.

translation.original_text: <sup>1</sup>κε ο θϲ μου δοξαϲω ϲε · ϋμνηϲω το ονομα ϲου · οτι εποιηϲαϲ θαυμαϲτα πραγματα · βουλην αρχεαν αληθινην γενοιτο κε . <sup>2</sup>οτι εθηκαϲ πολιν ειϲ χωμα · πολιϲ οχυραϲ · του πεϲιν αυτων τα θεμελια των αϲεβων · πολιϲ το αιωνα ˙ ου μη οικοδομηθη · <sup>3</sup>δια τουτο . ευλογηϲι ϲε ο λαοϲ ο πτωχοϲ · και πολιϲ ανθρωπων αδικουμενων ευλογηϲουϲιν ϲε · <sup>4</sup>εγενου γαρ παϲη πολι ταπινω βοηθοϲ · και τοιϲ αθυμηϲαϲιν δι εδιαν ϲκεπη · απο ανθρωπων πονηρων ρυϲη αυτουϲ · ϲκεπη διψωντω και πναʼ ανων > αδικουμενων . <sup>5</sup>ευλογηϲουϲιν ϲε . ωϲ ανθρωποι ολιγοψυχοι διψωντεϲ εν ϲιων οτι ρυϲη αυτουϲ απο ανων αϲεβων . οιϲ ημαϲ παρεδωκαϲ ˙
translation.text:
<sup>1</sup>O LORD, thou
art my God; I will exalt thee, I will praise thy name; for thou hast done wonderful
things; thy counsels of old
are faithfulness
and truth. <sup>2</sup>For thou hast made of a city an heap;
of a defenced city a ruin: a palace of strangers to be no city; it shall never be built. <sup>3</sup>Therefore shall the strong people glorify thee, the city of the terrible nations shall fear thee. <sup>4</sup>For thou hast been a strength to the poor, a strength to the needy in his distress, a refuge from the storm, a shadow from the heat, when the blast of the terrible ones
is as a storm
against the wall. <sup>5</sup>Thou shalt bring down the noise of strangers, as the heat in a dry place;
even the heat with the shadow of a cloud: the branch of the terrible ones shall be brought low.

translation.original_text: <sup>6</sup>και ποιηϲι κϲ ϲαβαωθʼ παϲιν τοιϲ εθνεϲιν ˙ επι το οροϲ τουτο πιονται ευφροϲυνην · πιονται οινον · χριϲονται μυρον <sup>7</sup>εν τω ορι τουτω ˙ παραδοϲ παντα ταυτα τοιϲ εθνεϲιν · η γαρ βουλη αυτη . επι παντα τα εθνη <sup>8</sup>κατεπιεν ο θανατοϲ ϊϲχυϲαϲ · και παλιν αφιλεν κϲ ο θϲ παν δακριον · απο παντοϲ προϲωπου ˙ το ονιδοϲ του λαου αφιλεν · απο παϲηϲ τηϲ γηϲ · το γαρ ϲτομα κυ · ελαληϲεν · <sup>9</sup>και ερουϲιν τη ημερα εκινη · ϊδου κϲ ο θϲ ημω · εφ ω ηλπιζομε · και ηκαλιωμεθα επι τω ϲωτηρι ημων και ευφανθηϲομεθα · <sup>10</sup>αβιτιϲ · ον τροπο πατουϲιν αλωνα εν αμαξαιϲ ·
translation.text:
<sup>6</sup>And in this mountain shall the LORD of hosts make unto all people a feast of fat things, a feast of wines on the lees, of fat things full of marrow, of wines on the lees well refined. <sup>7</sup>And he will destroy in this mountain the face of the covering cast over all people, and the vail that is spread over all nations. <sup>8</sup>He will swallow up death in victory; and the Lord GOD will wipe away tears from off all faces; and the rebuke of his people shall he take away from off all the earth: for the LORD hath spoken
it. <sup>9</sup>And it shall be said in that day, Lo, this
is our God; we have waited for him, and he will save us: this
is the LORD; we have waited for him, we will be glad and rejoice in his salvation. <sup>10</sup>For in this mountain shall the hand of the LORD rest, and Moab shall be trodden down under him, even as straw is trodden down for the dunghill.

translation.original_text: <sup>11</sup>και ανηϲει ταϲ χιραϲ αυτου · ον τροπον και αυτοϲ εταπινωϲεν του απολεϲαι · και ταπινωϲι την ϋβριν αυτου · εφ ην ταϲ χιραϲ επεβαλεν · <sup>12</sup>και το ϋψοϲ τηϲ καταφυγηϲ του τοιχου ϲου · ταπινωϲει · και καταβηϲεται εωϲ του εδαφουϲ :
translation.text:
<sup>11</sup>And he shall spread forth his hands in the midst of them, as he that swimmeth spreadeth forth
his hands to swim: and he shall bring down their pride together with the spoils of their hands. <sup>12</sup>And the fortress of the high fort of thy walls shall he bring down, lay low,
and bring to the ground,
even to the dust.
